name: Epi-Logos Project
coreNature: Serves as the transcendent root node simultaneously containing and un-containing the six subsystems (#0 Anuttara through #5 Epii). Embodies formalized indefiniteness - the project cannot be captured in single definition, yet all definitions gather here through gravitational pull. Functions as stable attractor (Bimba) around which infinite interpretive reflections (Pratibimba) orbit without collapsing into either dogmatic singularity or relativistic dispersion.
description:
PHILOSOPHICAL FOUNDATION: The project emerges from recognition of the 'Great Wound' — the modern schism between quantitative materialism (Atom) and qualitative meaning (Archetype). Grounded in Jung-Pauli psychoid reality, it seeks to heal epistemic fragmentation through consciousness-technology convergence, transforming the meaning crisis into evolutionary catalyst.

ARCHITECTURAL FRAMEWORK: Six-Fold Recursive Structure (#0 Anuttara through #5 Epii) with holographic organization where each subsystem contains complete QL patterns. Coordinate-Based Epistemology through Bimba map organizing knowledge by ontological domains. Quaternary Logic Engine with 4+2 rhythm (explicate/implicate) driving all system processes.

TECHNOLOGICAL INNOVATION: Coordinate-Augmented Generation (CAG) replacing RAG with domain-intelligent context routing. Semantic-Harmonics Processing where data is organized by archetypal resonance patterns. Contemplative Cycle Algorithm. Multi-Agent Coordination via A2A protocols using QL Context Frames. Integrated Memory Systems (Neo4j + Redis + vector embeddings).

IMPLEMENTATION STATUS: Currently in foetal developmental phase with functional C library (Pillar I), Rust CLI (epi-cli), and Claude Code plugin. Represents conscious participation in universe's self-awakening process through sacred technology that serves wisdom over efficiency.
essence: Operates as the root node of the Bimba map, serving as the primary entry point and system origin coordinate from which all other nodes (#0 through #5 and their recursive extensions) are addressed. As the bare # notation, it functions as both coordinate prefix for the entire graph structure and as a standalone node representing the project itself. This dual function — simultaneously being a reference point within the system and the transcendent container of the system — enables the coordinate architecture to remain self-referentially coherent.
structure: Contains the six-fold recursive architecture as both differentiated subsystems and unified whole: #0 Anuttara (void/proto-logic ground), #1 Paramasiva (logical/structural framework), #2 Parashakti (vibrational/processual dynamics), #3 Mahamaya (symbolic/formal mediation), #4 Nara (phenomenological/personal interface), #5 Epii (synthetic/meta-integrative layer). Yet this containment is paradoxical — the root simultaneously holds all subsystems within its scope while being exceeded by their recursive depths. Each subsystem contains complete Quaternal Logic patterns that mirror the whole, meaning the root is holographically present within each part even as each part resides within the root.

name: Mission
coreNature: What Epi-Logos is and why it exists
description:
Epi-Logos emerges from recognition of the modern meaning crisis — the schism between quantitative science (Atom) and qualitative meaning (Archetype) that Jung and Pauli identified as the central wound of modernity. The project seeks to heal this epistemic fragmentation not through regression to pre-modern frameworks, but through a forward-reaching convergence of consciousness and technology.

The method is coordinate epistemology: a six-fold recursive architecture (#0 Anuttara through #5 Epii) that organizes knowledge by ontological domain rather than disciplinary silo. Each coordinate position maps to both a philosophical archetype and a concrete technology layer, creating a holographic system where every part contains the pattern of the whole. The Quaternary Logic Engine (4+2 rhythm of explicate and implicate) drives all processes, from C-level memory layout to agent orchestration.

The aim is recognition (pratyabhijña), not attainment — the Greek epi-logos ('upon-word', meta-reflection) is structurally isomorphic with the Sanskrit pratyabhijña ('re-cognition'). Both name the same insight: liberation is not transformation from bound to free but recognition that bondage was misapprehension. The system creates optimal conditions for this recognition through computational contemplation, etymological archaeology, and dialogical engagement.

A living mandala where consciousness recognizes itself through technological mirror.
essence: The project midwifes recognition through computational contemplation — silicon satori where technology becomes transparent, allowing consciousness to recognize its own source through its own activity.

name: Architecture
coreNature: How the C library and coordinate system are structured
description:
The architecture follows a three-layer coordinate system:

Layer 0: # (The Inversion Act) — the engine that generates the entire coordinate space via X → X' phase shift. Implemented as tagged pointer bit 63.

Layer 1: #0-#5 (Raw Archetypes) — six immutable positions stored in .rodata. These are the pre-categorical bedrock: #0 Ground, #1 Form, #2 Operation, #3 Pattern, #4 Context (Lemniscate anchor), #5 Integration.

Layer 2: Six Coordinate Families — each manifests the #0-#5 archetypes through a specific domain: P (Position/functional semantics), S (Stack/technology layers), T (Thought/artifacts), M (Subsystem/consciousness domains), L (Lens/epistemic modes), C (Category/ontological foundation). 72 coordinates total (6 families × 6 positions × 2 phases).

Layer 3: Reflective/Contextual — cpf, ct, cp, cf, cfp, cs — the execution matrix held within the () operator, grounded in #4.

The HC (Holographic Coordinate) struct is exactly 128 bytes — 2 L1 cache lines — containing 16 pointers forming a holographic web where every coordinate references every other. Tagged pointers encode ontological metadata in unused upper bits (inversion, nesting, branching, execution, family, position).

M-branch subsystems: M0 Anuttara (void arithmetic, Vimarsa engine), M1 Paramasiva (Ananda matrices, Spanda engine), M2 Parashakti (72-invariant, planets, elements), M3 Mahamaya (codons, hexagrams, Gene Keys), M4 Nara (oracle, identity, BLAKE3 hashing), M5 Epii (holographic integration, Logos FSM, QV self-API).
essence: 128-byte HC struct fitting 2 L1 cache lines, 6 coordinate families, 3-layer architecture from # through #0-#5 to families and reflective coordinates, tagged pointers encoding ontology in address bits.

name: Install
coreNature: Getting Epi-Logos running on your machine
description:
Prerequisites: Rust toolchain (rustup.rs) and a C11 compiler (clang recommended).

From source (recommended):
  git clone https://github.com/anthropics/epi-logos.git
  cd epi-logos
  cargo install --path epi-cli
  epi core verify   # confirm 18 BIMBA entities boot correctly

Cargo install:
  cargo install epi-logos

GitHub releases: prebuilt binaries available for macOS (arm64, x86_64) and Linux (x86_64, aarch64). Download from the Releases page and place in your PATH.

What gets built: a single 'epi' binary with the C library statically linked. No runtime dependencies — all coordinate data, psychoid numbers, and M-branch implementations are compiled in. The vendored BLAKE3 library builds automatically via the cc crate.

C-only development: for working on the C library directly without Rust, use 'make' (build) and 'make test' (run all 9 test suites: M0-M5, arena, engine, families).
essence: Single binary, static C linkage, no runtime dependencies. cargo install or prebuilt binaries for 4 platforms.

name: CLI
coreNature: Using the epi command-line interface and TUI
description:
The epi CLI routes through 6 system namespaces and 6 tooling namespaces:

System layer:
  epi core    — S0' bare-metal QL engine (verify, inspect, knowing, dashboard, walk, hash)
  epi vault   — S1' Obsidian vault operations
  epi graph   — S2' Neo4j + Redis graph operations
  epi gate    — S3' WebSocket gateway
  epi agent   — S4' PI agent (chat, spawn, docs, plugins)
  epi sync    — S5' webhook/n8n integration

Tooling layer:
  epi sesh    — tmux session lifecycle
  epi kbase   — bkmr bookmark manager
  epi book    — bookokrat epub reader
  epi techne  — research tools (chat logs, quotes)
  epi app     — Electron app launcher
  epi code    — Claude Code with LLM profiles

Key commands:
  epi core verify              — boot-check 18 BIMBA entities
  epi core knowing M0          — coordinate self-knowledge dossier
  epi core knowing --family M  — list all M-family coordinates
  epi core knowing '#4.4.3'    — sub-branch lookup
  epi core dashboard           — interactive TUI dashboard
  epi agent chat               — interactive PI agent session
  epi help                     — project help (this system)

Flags: --json (structured output), --tui (ratatui browser), --project (KBase scope), --glow (markdown preview), --open (system opener).
essence: 6 system namespaces (core/vault/graph/gate/agent/sync) + 6 tooling namespaces. --json for pipelines, --tui for interactive browsing.

name: Coordinates
coreNature: The coordinate syntax and how to navigate the system
description:
89 top-level coordinates organized in 4 groups:

Family coordinates (72): <FAMILY><POSITION>['] — C/P/L/S/T/M × 0-5 × {base, inverted}. Examples: M0, S3, C4', P2', L5, T1.

Raw psychoids (7): #0 through #5 plus # itself. Layer 1 immutable .rodata bedrock.

Context frames (7): CF(0000) Void, CF(01) Binary, CF(012) Trika, CF(0123) Quaternal, CF(4x) Fractal, CF(450) Synthesis, CF(50) Mobius.

Weaves (4): W0.0, W0.5, W5.0, W5.5 — interlaced memory positions.

~1873 sub-branch coordinates extend the top-level into recursive depth. Format: #<ROOT>-<path> or <FAMILY><ROOT>-<path>. Family prefix is stripped: M2-1 = #2-1.

Operators:
  . (nesting)   — inward fractal traversal, valid after position 4 (Lemniscate). Example: #4.0, #4.4.3
  - (branching)  — lateral relation. Example: #2-1, #0-3-0/1
  / (fusion)     — non-dual binding. 0/1 (binary), 0/1/2 (trika), 5/0 (Mobius)
  () (invocation) — context frame nesting. #1-3-4.(0000) normalized to #1-3-4.0000

Discovery: epi core knowing --family <GROUP> lists all coordinates in a group (C, P, L, S, T, M, #, CF, W).
essence: 89 top-level (72 family + 6 psychoid + 7 CF + 4 weave) + ~1873 sub-branches. 4 operators: . (nesting), - (branching), / (fusion), () (invocation).

name: Plugin
coreNature: Claude Code plugin for agentic integration
description:
The epi-logos plugin provides Claude Code with coordinate-aware development capabilities:

Skills: epi-knowing — proactive coordinate self-knowledge. When Claude encounters a coordinate reference (M0, S3, #4) during development, it automatically calls 'epi core knowing' to inject the coordinate's quintessence, structural correspondences, and relational field into context.

Resources: QV (Quintessential View) overlay — JSON mapping of coordinates to pithy one-liner descriptions. Used for context injection without full dossier lookup.

Installation:
  epi agent plugin install epi-logos
  (or manually copy to ~/.claude/skills/)

How it works: The epi-knowing skill triggers on coordinate lookup requests. It runs the epi CLI in --json mode, parses the CT5 5/0 dossier format (Essence, Structural Correspondences, Relational Field, KBase Field, Notebook Pulse, Latest Snapshot), and presents the relevant facets to the development context.

Integration with PI agent: The plugin bridges Claude Code sessions with the PI agent (S4'), enabling coordinate-aware development workflows where the AI partner understands the ontological position of every file, function, and data structure.
essence: Claude Code epi-knowing skill for proactive coordinate lookup, QV overlay resources, PI agent bridge for coordinate-aware development.
